div.head: Mr. Adams to Mr. Seward

opener.dateline: Legation of the United States, London, October 12, 1867.

div.p: Sir: I have this morning received letters from Colonel Nagle and Captain Warren, announcing that they stand committed for trial by a commission to be held in Dublin on the 25th instant. They both apply to me for pecuniary aid in employing counsel for their defence. Under the instructions I have received, I shall venture to assume the responsibility of authorizing Mr. West to engage counsel to appear on their behalf.
I have the honor to be, sir, your obedient servant,

postscript.p: Hon. William H. Seward, Secretary of State, Washington, D. C.
